title: Tinospora bakis
intro: Tinospora bakis, also known as Guduchi or Amrita, is a woody climber belonging to the family Menispermaceae, native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. The plant is prized for its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties, which are said to aid in the prevention and treatment of various diseases such as fever, fatigue, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Tinospora bakis include alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, Tinospora bakis is commonly prepared in the form of a decoction or a powder, and is also used in the manufacture of herbal tablets, capsules, and other formulations.
taxonomy: Tinospora bakis is classified under the family Menispermaceae and is a species of flowering plant. It is native to tropical regions and is also known as Guduchi or Amrita. The plant is a climber and has various medicinal properties. Tinospora bakis is commonly found in India, Southeast Asia, and other parts of the world with a similar climate. It is often used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.

constituents: Tinospora bakis contains several active constituents including alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The alkaloids present in Tinospora bakis, such as berberine and palmatine, have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The combination of these active constituents makes Tinospora bakis a valuable plant with a range of medicinal applications.
parts: Tinospora bakis, also known as Giloy, is a plant used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine.
